description: Letter. The hand and style resemble those of Abū Zikrī the son of Eliyyahu the Judge. In Judaeo-Arabic, high register, rhymed prose. The contents are very difficult and need further examination. The writer seems to be defending himself against a rebuke he received from the addressee. He mentions his mother several times as well as a woman in labor, but the latter might be proverbial ("I hear that a woman in labor (makhīḍa) when nothing happens becomes enraged (maghīẓa).") He conveys good wishes for the addressee's recovery from an illness (r23–25). ASE.
tags: Abu Zikri al-Rayyis, illness letter 969-1517, illness: marad, Illness
description_he: מכתב. הכתב והסגנון דומים לאבו זכרי בן אליהו השופט. ביהודית-ערבית, סגנון גבוה, פרוזה מחורזת. הכותב מתגונן בפני תוכחה שקיבל. מזכיר את אמו ואישה יולדת. שולח ברכות להחלמת הנמען ממחלה.
